Restaurant · Bar · Inn — Since 1990

Montauk the way
it used to be.

An oceanfront restaurant on the bluffs of Old Montauk Highway. Local seafood, prime steaks, the Atlantic in every window — and a few rooms upstairs for those who'd rather not drive home.

ClosedWednesdays
An Oceanfront Restaurant · Family Owned

First a restaurant. Then a place to stay the night. Always with the ocean at the door.

Surfside Inn & Restaurant has been family owned for thirty-four years — best known to locals and travelers alike for what comes out of the kitchen. Long before that, the building stood as the Surf & Sand and the Anzac House, and during World War II served as billets for Coast Guard officers stationed along the coast.

The restaurant fills nightly with diners who came for the food and the view of the Atlantic. Upstairs, a handful of light, ocean-facing rooms wait for the guests who decide to stay.

34Years Family Run
1990Restaurant Opened

685 Old Montauk Hwy
“
Freshly prepared local seafood, prime steaks, and the chef's specialties — well known to locals and travelers alike.
— On Dining at Surfside
The Kitchen

Local seafood, prime steaks, and the menu the boats decide.

The dining room sits at the bluff's edge with the Atlantic in every window. White linens, blue glass, a quiet hum of conversation. In the warmer months the patio opens beneath striped umbrellas, and the bar — anchors painted on, blue stools, an honest pour — runs happy hour from four-thirty.

The menu changes with what the boats bring in. Local seafood, prime steaks, and a rotating list of house specialties from the kitchen.

SH
Executive Chef Shawn Hewitt Leads The Kitchen

The Property

The bar, the deck, the porch.

The Bar

Anchors, blue stools, an honest pour.

The Patio

Striped umbrellas at the bluff's edge.

The Porch

Hydrangeas, white railings, the Atlantic just beyond.

Stay The Night

And, a few rooms upstairs.

Light, naturally decorated, every one overlooking the Atlantic — open the windows and you'll hear the surf. Spacious, air-conditioned, with private baths, refrigerators, coffeemakers, and a beach path a few yards from the front door.

It's a quieter kind of stay. Beach umbrellas, chairs, and towels are kept by the door, and the village is a five-minute walk down the bluff.

Ocean View Private Bath A/C · TV · WiFi Refrigerator
From $225 Sun – Thu, May rates
Reserve a Room

Adults only · Two guests per room · Two-night minimum · Stay three nights, Sun–Thu, your fourth is on us.

A Note On The Sign

The crab on the gate has been here longer than most things in town.

The path to the ocean is just beyond the front porch. A short walk down the shoreline — or along the paved path — and you're in Montauk Village.

It's a particular kind of quiet you find here. Different from the rest of the East End. Older.

— Montauk the way it used to be.
